macro_segments.title: Review of Previous Story: Rabbi Elazar son of Rabbi Shimon — His Afflictions and Death

micro_segments.title: Summary of the magistrate episode and Rabbi Elazar's self-imposed afflictions
The preservation of Rabbi Elazar's body for 18-22 years and burial in his father's cave

macro_segments.title: Rabbi's Proposal to Rabbi Elazar's Widow and the Comparison of Their Merit

micro_segments.title: Rabbi's marriage proposal to Rabbi Elazar's wife and her response comparing them as vessels
The parable of the owner's weapons and the shepherd's pouch
Rabbi's rebuttal about his superiority in good deeds despite her claims
Rabbi Elazar's greater merit in accepting suffering; the widow's final response

macro_segments.title: The Story of Young Rabbi and Rabbi Elazar: Scholastic Rank and Promotion

micro_segments.title: Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel and Rabbi Yehoshua ben Korcha as chief teachers with young rabbis beneath them
The young students asking questions and answering, prompting the promotion of Rabbi and Rabbi Elazar to stools
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel's statement about his one dove among them and concern over the evil eye
Rabbi Yehoshua ben Korcha's question about protection and demotion of both Rabbi and Rabbi Elazar
Rabbi Elazar's despondency at being demoted because of Rabbi
Rabbi Elazar ceasing to support Rabbi's questions after the demotion
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel's comfort to Rabbi: Elazar is a lion son of a lion, you are a lion son of a fox

macro_segments.title: Three Exemplars of Humility: Rabban Shimon, Bnei Beteira, and Yonatan ben Shaul

micro_segments.title: Rabbi's declaration of three modest people: his father, Bnei Beteira, and Yonatan ben Shaul
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel taking the insult upon himself
Bnei Beteira recognizing Hillel's superiority and ceding power
Yonatan ben Shaul accepting David's kingship
Gemara questioning whether these were truly humble or just pragmatic

macro_segments.title: Rabbi's Afflictions Compared to Rabbi Elazar's: Voluntary Suffering vs. Consequential Suffering

micro_segments.title: Rabbi's declaration that afflictions are beloved and his acceptance of thirteen years of suffering
Description of Rabbi's afflictions: urinary infection and oral disease for specified periods
Ahuroira's management of feeding animals to time with Rabbi's bathroom use
The power of Rabbi's pain overwhelming the noise of horses and reaching sailors
Comparison: Rabbi Elazar's afflictions came and left from love; Rabbi's came and left from deed

macro_segments.title: The Story of the Calf: Why Rabbi's Afflictions Came Upon Him

micro_segments.title: A calf runs away to Rabbi's skirts and cries; Rabbi shows no compassion
Heavenly decree that Rabbi should suffer so he learns compassion
Rabbi shows compassion to weasels and the heavenly response to show compassion on him
The blessing during Rabbi's afflictions: no need for rain and the ground filled with water

macro_segments.title: Rabbi's Intervention: The Son of Rabbi Elazar ben Rabbi Shimon Goes Astray and is Saved

micro_segments.title: Rabbi comes to the village of Rabbi Elazar and learns his son became a male prostitute
Rabbi gives the son semichah (ordination) and places him with Rabbi Shimon ben Isi ben Lekonya
The son initially resists learning but is convinced by the honor of his position
The son grows up and becomes a talmid chacham; Rabbi recognizes his voice
Rabbinic citation: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life; the one who takes souls is wise
When Rabbi Yosi dies, the serpent refuses to let him into his father's cave
Heavenly voice explains the father suffered in the cave; the son did not

macro_segments.title: Rabbi's Intervention: The Grandson of Rabbi Tarfon Goes Astray and is Saved

micro_segments.title: Rabbi comes to Rabbi Tarfon's village and learns of his grandson who is a male prostitute
Rabbi offers his daughter to the grandson if he does teshuvah
Dispute: some say he married and divorced; others say he refused to marry to avoid appearance

macro_segments.title: Rabbinic Principle: Teaching Your Friend's Son Torah — Its Ultimate Reward

micro_segments.title: Why does Rabbi go to such effort? Teaching a friend's son Torah brings one to the heavenly academy
Teaching the son of an ignoramus Torah — God annuls the decree for your sake
Principle: if you are a talmid chacham, your son and grandson are, Torah never leaves your line

macro_segments.title: Stories of Fasting and Merit: Rav Yosef, Rav Zeira, and the Guarantee of Torah in the Family

micro_segments.title: Rav Yosef fasts forty days to reach the promise that Torah will not leave his mouth
Another forty fasts to include his children; a third fast to include grandchildren
Once he achieves three generations, Torah returns to its lodging place
Rav Zeira fasts a hundred days to forget Babylonian learning upon arriving in Eretz Yisrael
Rashi explains: Eretz Yisrael learning is more focused on peshat; Babylonian learning is more analytical
Maharsha critiques purposeless pilpul (casuistry) that values winning arguments over truth
Another hundred fasts so Rabbi Elazar does not die while he is alive
Another hundred fasts that the fire of Gehinnom should not affect him
Rav Zeira tests himself monthly in a hot oven; the rabbis give him the evil eye and burn his legs

macro_segments.title: Why Was the Land Destroyed? The Importance of Blessings Over Torah Learning

micro_segments.title: Wise people, prophets could not explain why the land was destroyed until God said it himself
Rav Yehuda in Rav's name: they did not make the blessings of Torah first
Connection to three-generation blessing: blessings express the value of Torah as a gift

macro_segments.title: Three Teachings on Humility and Torah Study in This World and the World to Come

micro_segments.title: Reish Lakish on small and great: those who humble themselves in Torah become great in the world to come
Rav Chama: wisdom rests in the heart of the wise, but is made known among fools
Ulla's parable: one coin in a pitcher makes noise; many coins are silent
Those who make themselves servants to Torah in this world become free in the world to come

macro_segments.title: Reish Lakish's Search for Rabbi Chiya's Cave: The Importance of Spreading Torah

micro_segments.title: Reish Lakish marks the caves of the rabbis in Tzfat but cannot find Rabbi Chiya's cave
Reish Lakish becomes despondent, questioning whether he was not as sharp in Torah analysis
Heavenly voice: you were as sharp as he, but you did not spread Torah like he did
Introduction to Rabbi Chiya's power in spreading Torah (story to continue)
